seoTitle: How to Import a CSV File into ClickUp
metaDescription: Learn how to prepare and map a CSV spreadsheet for import into ClickUp, matching columns to task fields and workspace members correctly.
intro: Migrating an existing spreadsheet of tasks into ClickUp doesn't have to mean re-entering everything by hand. This guide covers formatting your CSV file so its columns align with ClickUp's structure, using the native import wizard to map headers like task name and due date, and matching external user emails to workspace members so assignees carry over correctly.

steps.heading: Format your CSV spreadsheet
steps.body: Structure your rows and specify clear column headers, like Task Name, Due Date, and Priority, so they're easy to map later.

steps.heading: Open the Import & Export settings
steps.body: Click your profile avatar to open Settings, then navigate to the Import & Export section and click Start Import.

steps.heading: Select the CSV format
steps.body: Choose the comma-separated value option and drag and drop your saved spreadsheet file into the upload window.

steps.heading: Choose your destination
steps.body: Select the target Space, Folder, or List where the imported tasks should land.

steps.heading: Map your columns
steps.body: Use the interactive mapping grid to link each spreadsheet column to the correct ClickUp field or custom property.

steps.heading: Match assignees and verify the import
steps.body: Map user emails to workspace accounts so task assignees transfer correctly, then review the success screen to confirm your list populated as expected.

faq.question: What happens if a user's email in my CSV doesn't match a workspace member?
faq.answer: That task's assignee field likely won't transfer, so it's worth double-checking your CSV emails against your workspace member list before importing.

faq.question: Should I test with a small file first?
faq.answer: Yes, running a small sample import is a good way to confirm your column mapping works before importing a large spreadsheet.

faq.question: Can I import into an existing list, or only a new one?
faq.answer: You can choose your target destination during the import process, which can be an existing Space, Folder, or List.

faq.question: Does ClickUp support Excel files as well as CSV?
faq.answer: The import wizard is built around CSV formatting, so Excel files typically need to be saved as CSV before importing, unless a direct Excel option is offered.
